title: Serialization event handlers should be implemented correctly
description: Serialization event handlers should be implemented correctlyAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoSerialization event handlers that don’t have the correct signature will not be called, bypassing augmentations to automated serialization and deserialization events.A method is designated a serialization event handler by applying one of the following serialization event attributes:OnSerializingAttributeOnSerializedAttributeOnDeserializingAttributeOnDeserializedAttributeSerialization event handlers take a single parameter of typeStreamingContext, returnvoid, and haveprivatevisibility.This rule raises an issue when any of these constraints are not respected.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub,
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Serialization event handler with incorrect signature

conditions: Method signature violates requirements: not private, not void return, or wrong parameters

exclusion_conditions: Methods with correct signature (private void Method(StreamingContext))
never_trigger_on: Methods with correct signature: private void MethodName(StreamingContext context)
logic.reasoning: Serialization event handlers must follow specific signature requirements to be invoked by the runtime. Wrong visibility (not private), wrong return type (not void), or wrong parameters (not StreamingContext) will cause handlers to be silently ignored. Uses custom function to validate all signature requirements.
